tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment, and metabolic acidosis (low CO₂, high chloride). Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glycosuria, which may suggest recent hypoglycemia or analytical interference. Recommend further renal and metabolic evaluation and clinical correlation.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
